Who I am

Curious mind,
purposeful builder.

I'm Solomon Ongar a Computer Science student at Uganda Christian University with a genuine obsession for understanding how systems work. Not just how to use them, but why they work the way they do: from configuring OSPF routing across multi-site enterprise networks, to building adaptive ed-tech that runs over USSD for students who have no internet.

My technical work is grounded in real constraints. UGscholar a product I co-built as part of a five-person team was designed specifically for Uganda's 74% without internet access, running entirely over feature phones via *289#. That kind of engineering challenge, working within real limits to reach real people, is what motivates me most.

Beyond building, I document. I write on Medium and LinkedIn because I believe the most powerful thing a junior developer can do is make their learning visible. Every workshop, every debugging session, every configuration I fix becomes a post — because someone else is stuck on the same thing right now.

Academic Journey

The schools that
shaped how I think.

Three institutions, each building a different layer of discipline and curiosity.

2023 — Present
Uganda Christian University
Bachelor of Science — Computer Science

Core focus on algorithms, data structures, systems programming, and networking. Completed intensive practical recess programmes — configuring multi-VLAN Cisco networks, writing LaTeX technical documentation, and co-building a real USSD-based ed-tech product with a cross-functional team.

2021 — 2022
Makerere College School
A-Level — Uganda Advanced Certificate of Education

One of Uganda's most selective secondary schools. Developed the analytical rigour and disciplined study habits that carry directly into engineering work today.

2017 — 2020
Seeta High School
O-Level — Uganda Certificate of Education

Strong foundation in Mathematics, Sciences, and English. The intellectual curiosity cultivated here started the journey.

Portfolio

Things I've built.

Real projects with real constraints — from campus networks to ed-tech for feature phones.

Featured — 01
UGscholar — Adaptive Learning Platform

Uganda's first adaptive learning platform built for all phones — no internet required. Students dial *289# on any MTN or Airtel line to access the full P1–S4 curriculum in 5 local languages. An AI gap-detection engine identifies each learner's weak spots in real time. Parental progress reports delivered via SMS. Built for Uganda's 74% without internet access.

USSD SMS Gateway Node.js PostgreSQL Africa's Talking Adaptive Learning MTN & Airtel APIs
0 MB
Data required to learn
5
Local languages supported
P1–S4
Full Uganda NCDC curriculum
20M+
MTN/Airtel subscriber reach
02
UCU Multi-Site Enterprise Network

Designed and deployed a complete 8-rack campus network — 13 VLANs, router-on-a-stick inter-VLAN routing, DHCP, SSH v2 hardening, and OSPF Area 0. Verified live with full show command output and end-to-end pings across racks.

Cisco IOS OSPF VLANs DHCP SSH Packet Tracer
03
Student Records System

Python CLI application for managing student data — registration, grade tracking, and formatted report generation. Built with file I/O, modular design, and clean data structure patterns.

Python CLI File I/O Data Structures
04
Memory Allocator in C

A simplified dynamic memory allocator simulating malloc and free. Demonstrates deep understanding of heap management, pointer arithmetic, and systems-level programming in C.

C Systems Memory Pointers
05
Algorithm Benchmarker

Python scripts demonstrating and benchmarking sorting and searching algorithms — bubble sort, merge sort, binary search — with step-by-step output and real performance comparison data.

Python Algorithms DSA Benchmarks
